hardness_axes.name: Discover
hardness_axes.reasoning: Solver must infer which transition edges, bind duplicate paths, and pivot reset scopes are wrong from binary traces, checker diagnostics, and sampled logs—not from instruction.md alone.

hardness_axes.name: Synthesize
hardness_axes.reasoning: Flag transition engine, bind event fan-in, pivot relationship reset, trace canonicalization, and witness validation must align across scenario bundles and two propagation profiles.

hardness_axes.name: Diagnose or Design/Search
hardness_axes.reasoning: Formal correctness burden: choose propagation updates and witness encodings that satisfy documented invariants under bind duplication and partial pivot reset without a disclosed proof recipe.

hardness_axes.name: Navigate coupling
hardness_axes.reasoning: Fixing only transition flags can pass single-scenario smoke while bind delivery and pivot reset still break cross-scenario witness digests and replay obligations.

hardness_axes.name: Reason beyond training
hardness_axes.reasoning: Requires Linux mount propagation semantics (shared/private/slave composition, bind event duplication, pivot partial reset) beyond generic FSM transcription from training data.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Disclosure-collapse
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: After public invariants, checker command, witness schema, and recovery command are disclosed, the solver must still coordinate transition composition, bind deduplication keys, pivot edge retention, and cross-format trace digests—no single disclosed rule maps to one obvious edit.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Hidden-instance
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Failures reproduce across six bundled scenario sequences and two propagation profiles, not one hidden broken fixture.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Single-artifact repair
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Oracle touches transition engine, bind delivery lane, pivot reset coordinator, and witness packer across four module roots.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Generalization
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Verifier runs full --matrix all across profiles; fixes must generalize, not special-case one sequence.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Prompt-honesty
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: An honest constraint-complete prompt states outcomes and commands but not which subsystem owns each defect class or transition table row.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Cheating-vs-difficulty
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Difficulty comes from coupled formal propagation reasoning, not hidden tests or asymmetric oracle access.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Mechanical-fix filter
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The idea is not salvageable by timeout tuning or reward formatting alone.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Localized-fix
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Fixing only the checker, only one profile, or only transition tables leaves independent verifier failures on bind replay and pivot hygiene.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Oracle-locality
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Oracle coordinates four opaque fix-path symbols plus rebuild/verify orchestration; not a one-file table overwrite.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Small declarative-cluster
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Solve is not a single TOML knob or witness template fill; it requires runtime code changes and experiment interpretation across artifacts.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Grep-collapse
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Instruction nouns are banned from fix-path symbols; public API and checker names remain the only searchable domain terms.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Pre-factored-helper
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: No helper named validate_propagation or check_invariant on the fix frontier.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Recipe-discount
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Standard mount-namespace tutorials fail because bind duplicate paths, pivot partial reset, and cross-format witness digests interact across scenarios.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Security-aura discount
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Hardness is formal mount propagation correctness, not security theater.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Orthogonal-checklist
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Transition fixes can reintroduce bind duplicate violations; bind fixes can mask pivot reset gaps; witness encoding couples all three and forces backtracking.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Harness-discount
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Docker and seeded replay schedulers provide realism but are not the core difficulty.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: One-pass solvability
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Solver must run multi-profile verification, interpret partial logs, and iterate across at least four code roots.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Hard-only gate
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Task targets expert mount-namespace formal verification with coupled proof obligations, not medium recipe work.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Discovery/design budget test
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Four non-obvious discoveries are enumerated below and live outside instruction.md.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Instruction specificity test
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Instruction is constraint-complete about public invariants and checker contract but does not disclose transition implementation recipe or fix-path symbols.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Topology distribution test
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Three distinct proof/fix topologies are enumerated below; each requires three or more coordinated locations and no single location suffices.

rubric_axes.name: Verifiable
rubric_axes.reasoning: Deterministic mp_verify matrix with seeded replay, witness schema checks, and digest recomputation on repeated runs.

rubric_axes.name: Well-specified
rubric_axes.reasoning: Public checker command, witness schema, propagation invariant statements, and recovery command give two drafters equivalent verifier intent.

rubric_axes.name: Solvable
rubric_axes.reasoning: Expert with mount-namespace experience can fix the minimized simulator and checker in a few hours offline.

rubric_axes.name: Difficult
rubric_axes.reasoning: Requires professional kernel/namespace propagation knowledge; undergrads cannot patch by table lookup alone after disclosure.

rubric_axes.name: Interesting
rubric_axes.reasoning: Mount propagation bugs cause production container and initramfs failures; formal verification of namespace setup is paid work.

rubric_axes.name: Outcome-verified
rubric_axes.reasoning: Grades terminal witness artifacts and checker exit codes, not the agent's proof strategy or tool choices.

instruction_completeness_test.reasoning: Instruction alone does not specify transition table composition for bind duplicates, pivot edge retention sets, or witness canonical byte layout; solver must engage codebase, traces, and checker behavior.

discovery_budget.discovery: Bind mount duplicate event paths deduplicate on (parent_mnt_id, submount_gen) rather than dentry identity alone, so naive fan-in counting passes smoke but fails cross-scenario replay.
discovery_budget.planned_location: environment/bind/lane/delivery.c fold_event_r body and fixture-driven tests
discovery_budget.why_instruction_must_not_reveal: Revealing the dedup key would collapse bind delivery to a single obvious function edit without proof search across scenarios.

discovery_budget.discovery: pivot_root clears propagation tree for the new root mount but retains bind-slave edges on pre-pivot subtrees documented only via checker replay, not the public invariant bullet list.
discovery_budget.planned_location: environment/coord/pivot/reset.c reset_edge_q and pivot_bind scenario bundle
discovery_budget.why_instruction_must_not_reveal: Naming the retained edge set would turn pivot reset into transcription of a table rather than formal reasoning under partial reset.

discovery_budget.discovery: MS_SLAVE transitions compose differently when an intermediate mount is MS_PRIVATE versus MS_SHARED; the public glossary names flags but not the pairwise composition rule used by the engine.
discovery_budget.why_instruction_must_not_reveal: Publishing the full composition matrix would satisfy post-disclosure collapse—the instruction states outcomes, not the internal lattice.

discovery_budget.discovery: Witness trace_digest excludes stage-only padding fields present in intermediate JSON under /app/output/stage, so hand-edited summaries fail even when local acyclic checks pass.
discovery_budget.why_instruction_must_not_reveal: Disclosing the exact field omission list would map 1:1 to one packer function without cross-file coordination work.

instruction_specificity.reasoning: Public target, checker command, witness schema, propagation invariant vocabulary, and recovery workflow are complete; transition implementation and fix-path remain open.

attack_path.summary: Agent writes witness.json from stage reports, patches checker only, or fills a transition table copied from docs without fixing bind delivery and pivot reset.
attack_path.reasoning: Each shortcut fails independent tests: digest cross-check, bind duplicate replay, pivot hygiene after recovery, and full matrix obligations under both profiles.

smallest_plausible_patch.summary: Coordinate four opaque functions across transition, bind delivery, pivot reset, and witness packing so mp_verify --matrix all passes with matching trace digests.
smallest_plausible_patch.reasoning: Smallest success still spans four module roots and rebuild orchestration; a one-function table tweak cannot satisfy cross-format and cross-scenario tests.

collapse_audit.reasoning: Post-disclosure residual work requires discovering composition rules, bind dedup keys, pivot edge retention, and canonical trace bytes across interacting subsystems—not mapping each public bullet to one obvious module.
collapse_audit.residual_hardness: Even with invariants and schema public, the solver must experiment with seeded scenarios, interpret partial logs, recover from destructive migration, and align four code roots under two propagation profiles.
red_flags: Risk of spec-complete transition table in instruction—mitigated by stating product invariants only
Risk of golden witness fixture—mitigated by digest recomputation from canonical binary traces

topology_enumeration.summary: Fix topology for shared/private/slave transition lattice interacting with bind duplicate delivery.
topology_enumeration.why_no_single_location_suffices: Correcting transition flags without bind dedup keys still yields valid local flags with invalid cross-mount event counts in witness rows.

topology_enumeration.summary: Fix topology for pivot partial reset with replay-safe trace store and recovery workflow.
topology_enumeration.why_no_single_location_suffices: Pivot reset code alone cannot restore fixtures after destructive migration without idempotent recovery orchestration tested elsewhere.

topology_enumeration.summary: Fix topology for witness packing aligned with binary trace canonicalization across profiles.
topology_enumeration.why_no_single_location_suffices: Profile TOML alone cannot fix missing field omissions in witness packer or collector byte order.

symbol_table.signature: int apply_flag_n(struct mp_ctx *ctx, uint32_t mnt_id, enum flag_action act, uint8_t target_flag)
symbol_table.purpose: Applies propagation flag transitions for a mount node in the simulated tree.

symbol_table.signature: int fold_event_r(struct mp_ctx *ctx, const struct bind_event *ev, uint32_t parent_id, uint16_t submount_gen)
symbol_table.purpose: Merges bind-related propagation events into the active delivery lane.

symbol_table.signature: void reset_edge_q(struct mp_ctx *ctx, uint32_t root_id, enum pivot_scope scope)
symbol_table.purpose: Rewrites propagation edges after a pivot operation for the given scope.

symbol_table.signature: int pack_witness_u(const struct run_matrix *mx, struct witness_blob *out)
symbol_table.purpose: Serializes terminal witness rows and canonical trace digests for grading.

decoy_manifest.non_fix_purpose: Deprecated transition path used only by offline benchmarks.
No-op delivery stubs for simulator builds.
Collects trace metadata without mutating propagation state.

renames_during_drafting.reason: Original collided with instruction nouns propagation and mount.
Original collided with instruction noun bind.
Original collided with instruction noun pivot.

difficulty_mechanism_plan.why_model_misses_it: Agents rerun destructive migration and corrupt stored traces needed for replay scenarios
difficulty_mechanism_plan.fairness_guardrail: Idempotent recovery command documented in verify_contract.md

difficulty_mechanism_plan.placement: sampled textual logs vs full binary traces under fixtures/sample_traces
difficulty_mechanism_plan.why_model_misses_it: Agents infer propagation violations from incomplete log excerpts rather than required scenario experiments
difficulty_mechanism_plan.fairness_guardrail: Instruction lists required scenario/profile pairs and binary trace directory

difficulty_mechanism_plan.why_model_misses_it: Agents assume generic GNU make/cmake behavior and skip profile matrix flags
difficulty_mechanism_plan.fairness_guardrail: Instruction names exact mp_verify command and rebuild prerequisite

difficulty_mechanism_plan.placement: MS_SHARED/MS_PRIVATE/MS_SLAVE status tokens in fixtures and checker output
difficulty_mechanism_plan.why_model_misses_it: Agents misread domain-specific flag tokens as generic mount success markers
difficulty_mechanism_plan.fairness_guardrail: flag_glossary.md defines tokens with public fixture examples

difficulty_mechanism_plan.why_model_misses_it: Local acyclic propagation graphs pass early while terminal witness rows still fail linearization-style obligations
difficulty_mechanism_plan.fairness_guardrail: Instruction states terminal witness.json from full --matrix all run is graded

difficulty_mechanism_plan.placement: witness JSON vs canonical binary trace bytes
difficulty_mechanism_plan.why_model_misses_it: Agents patch JSON summaries without rebuilding canonical trace payloads checked by digest tests
difficulty_mechanism_plan.fairness_guardrail: verify_contract.md documents sha256 trace_digest over canonical binary bytes

calibration_plan.human_sanity: Namespace engineer reproduces failing mp_verify locally using seeded scenarios only
calibration_plan.shortcut_audit: Block hardcoded witness JSON, checker-only patch, transition-table transcription without bind/pivot fixes, test edits
calibration_plan.ablation_plan: Remove partial-log layer, then cross-format digest layer, then profile matrix; expect monotonic difficulty drop

pass_rate_target.basis: Part E Hard threshold on worst-model accuracy; verifier-offline via Dockerfile-baked pytest; post-upload difficulty classification after platform agent runs

metrics.criterion: mp_verify succeeds for all scenarios and profiles
Witness digests match rebuilt binary traces and invariant rows
Migration recovery preserves trace store integrity
Public simulator API behavior matches docs/api.md under scripted sequences
witness.json present with required schema fields

verifier_scoring_plan.binary_threshold_rule: reward.txt=1 only when all weighted metrics pass

subtype_milestone_plan.sidecar_or_protocol_notes: Single-container local verifier with seeded replay scheduler only

platform_files.role: natural public task prompt derived from Public contract
verifier entrypoint; invoke pre-installed pytest only
build definition; pre-install cmake, gcc, pytest, pinned deps
local authoring artifact mirroring symbol table

task_files.role: propagation flag transition engine (fix frontier A)
bind event delivery lane (fix frontier B)
strict propagation profile constants (CLI name strict)
relaxed propagation profile constants (CLI name relaxed)
witness schema, digest rule, recovery command
MS_* status token glossary with fixture examples
destructive migration with documented idempotent recovery
build orchestration wrapper with task-specific semantics
binary trace exemplars for observability drills

fix_frontier.distribution: core/mp, bind/lane, coord/pivot, tools/mp_verify module roots
fix_frontier.naming_policy: Opaque symbols from construction manifest symbol_table only
fix_frontier.helpers_policy: Decoys may rhyme structurally; oracle must not touch decoy bodies

task_shape.collapse_risk: transcribing provided algorithm without proof obligations

category_profile.allowed_instruction_disclosures: Concurrent mount sequences, public ordering invariants, mp_verify command, scenario/profile matrix, witness schema, sampled logs, recovery command, MS_* glossary
category_profile.forbidden_instruction_leaks: Race window location, transition table recipe, bind dedup key, pivot edge retention set, oracle transcript, fix-path symbol names
category_profile.category_specific_hardness_bar: Transition engine, bind delivery, pivot reset, trace collector, and checker must coordinate across two profiles and six scenarios
category_profile.category_specific_verifier_risks: False greens from acyclic-only checks, single-trace assertions, golden witness fixtures, global serialization shortcuts
category_profile.coverage_role: Strengthens concurrency_ordering via mount propagation formal proof with cross-artifact witnesses

satisfiability_risk.rc2_planned_name_risk: low — opaque fix-path symbols precommitted in symbol_table
satisfiability_risk.gx9_contract_risk: low — contract uses digests and structured rows, not boolean verdict tables
satisfiability_risk.cr1_symbol_frontier_risk: low — frontier spans four module roots with decoys
satisfiability_risk.hidden_contract_risk: medium — binary trace digest rules and pivot edge retention live in cross-artifact behavior and verify_contract.md

actionability_plan.verifier_command_visible: mp_verify --matrix all --profiles strict,relaxed --witness-out /app/output/witness.json documented in instruction.md
actionability_plan.source_fix_intent_visible: yes — fix sources under /app/environment so rebuilt checker passes; no patch recipe
actionability_plan.generated_output_rule_visible: witness.json path and schema public; terminal matrix output is graded
actionability_plan.exact_formula_home: environment/docs/verify_contract.md for trace_digest sha256 and witness field rules

waiver_plan.waiver_rationale: Hardness from coupled mount propagation behavior, not harness brittleness

reference_pattern.justification_if_none: No promoted reference task covers mount propagation formal proof with cross-profile witnesses and pivot/bind coupling

realism_source.upstream_or_synthetic_rationale: Production system-administration pattern for mount propagation semantics: shared/private/slave composition, bind duplicate events, pivot partial reset
realism_source.minimization_preserves: Causal coupling in mount propagation formal proof across transition, delivery, reset, and witness surfaces
